metadata.title: D7 Technology — Autonomous Systems & Advanced Sensing
metadata.description: D7 Technology designs intelligent autonomous platforms and advanced sensing technologies for real-world applications.

metadata.title: About D7 Technology
metadata.description: D7 Technology is a deep-tech R&D company specializing in autonomous vehicles, radar/sonar, AI/ML, sensor fusion, and wearable sensors. Based in Istanbul, Turkiye.

AboutPage.heroDescription: We are a deep-tech research and engineering company building the sensing and autonomy technologies that will define the next generation of intelligent systems.
AboutPage.missionTitle: Engineering intelligence into the physical world
AboutPage.missionP1: D7 Technology exists to close the gap between advanced research and real-world systems. We combine rigorous signal processing, modern machine learning, and engineering discipline to build platforms that operate reliably in demanding environments.
AboutPage.missionP2: Our work spans autonomous surface vessels, multi-static radar tracking, wearable sensor systems, and AI-driven perception — all grounded in peer-reviewed research and tested in the field.
AboutPage.missionP3: We operate at the frontier of deep technology — and we build to the standard where research meets deployment.

innovation.title: Innovation
innovation.desc: Pushing boundaries in sensing and autonomy through rigorous R&D and novel algorithmic approaches.

rigor.title: Rigor
rigor.desc: Rigorous standards in research methodology — from mathematical modeling to empirical field validation.

impact.title: Real-World Impact
impact.desc: Every project targets practical deployment — we build things that work outside the lab.

openScience.title: Open Science
openScience.desc: Contributing to the global research community through peer-reviewed publications and open collaboration.

metadata.title: ADVISOR: Autonomous Surface Ship for Guidance
metadata.description: EU ITEA Program — Escort-type Autonomous Surface Vehicle for cooperative navigation in constrained maritime environments.

AdvisorPage.title: ADVISOR: Autonomous Surface Ship for Guidance
AdvisorPage.subtitle: EU ITEA Program — Escort-type Autonomous Surface Vehicle
AdvisorPage.overviewText: ADVISOR is an escort-type Autonomous Surface Vehicle (ASV) developed under the EU ITEA program, focusing on autonomous guidance and cooperative navigation in constrained maritime environments including harbors and near-shore areas. The project emphasizes supervised autonomy — where human oversight coexists with automated decision-making — to enable safe, reliable operation in dynamic and cluttered waterways. Field validation is a central component of the program.
AdvisorPage.fieldTrialText: Field trials are conducted in representative maritime environments to validate navigation algorithms, sensor fusion performance, and autonomous decision-making under real operational conditions. The ASV undergoes structured test scenarios including convoy operations, obstacle avoidance, and return-to-base maneuvers.
AdvisorPage.fieldTrialCaption: ADVISOR field trial — autonomous surface vessel in harbor environment
AdvisorPage.fieldTrialImageAlt: Autonomous surface vehicle — Maritime Robotics platform for harbor and escort trials
AdvisorPage.navText: The ADVISOR platform integrates a comprehensive multi-modal sensor suite to achieve robust situational awareness in complex, dynamic maritime environments. Sensor data is fused in real time to support obstacle detection, path planning, and adaptive navigation.
AdvisorPage.bathyText1: In addition to navigation, ADVISOR collects environmental and oceanographic data during operations. Onboard meteorological sensors continuously monitor wind speed, wind direction, air temperature, and atmospheric pressure — providing contextual data that informs route planning and maneuverability analysis.
AdvisorPage.bathyText2: Bathymetric mapping capabilities support harbor surveying and shallow-water navigation, contributing to a richer environmental model that enhances the ASV's autonomous decision-making.
AdvisorPage.bathyCaption: Bathymetry and environmental data collection during field operations
AdvisorPage.bathyImageAlt: 3D bathymetric map on a display: color-coded seafloor depths with survey overlays during operations

metadata.title: Multi-Static Radar Systems
metadata.description: Spatially distributed radar for multi-target tracking and spatial diversity — IEEE RadarConf25 research by D7 Technology.

MultistaticPage.title: Multi-Static Radar Systems
MultistaticPage.subtitle: Spatially distributed radar for multi-target tracking and spatial diversity
MultistaticPage.overviewText: Multi-static radar uses separated transmitters and receivers to observe targets from multiple aspect angles, improving detection robustness for low-RCS (Radar Cross Section) targets and reducing sensitivity to clutter, interference, and stealth shaping. By exploiting spatial diversity, multi-static configurations overcome fundamental limitations of monostatic radar and provide richer measurement geometry for target tracking and classification.
MultistaticPage.simulationText: Simulation environments model the geometric distribution of radar nodes, target trajectories, and measurement uncertainty. Monte Carlo trials across diverse target scenarios are used to validate plot fusion algorithms and assess tracking performance metrics including detection probability, false alarm rate, and positional accuracy.
MultistaticPage.simulationCaption: Multi-static radar simulation map — node distribution and target tracking scenario
MultistaticPage.simulationImageAlt: Multi-panel simulation maps: atmospheric and polarimetric radar fields over west-east and south-north distance in kilometers
MultistaticPage.demoText: The following demonstration illustrates the multi-static radar system processing pipeline, including plot-level data fusion, track initiation, and multi-target tracking performance in a simulated scenario.

metadata.title: Wearable Sensors & Performance Analysis
metadata.description: UWB and IMU-based human motion tracking and performance analysis research by D7 Technology. IEEE SIU 2025 publication.

WearableSensorsPage.title: Wearable Sensors & Performance Analysis
WearableSensorsPage.subtitle: UWB and IMU-based human motion tracking and performance analysis
WearableSensorsPage.overviewText: Research into wearable sensor systems combining UWB (Ultra-Wideband) and IMU (Inertial Measurement Unit) technologies for precise human motion tracking, fall detection, and sports performance analysis. This project spans controlled lab environments and real-world field experiments.
WearableSensorsPage.localizationText: A structured indoor test environment was established to validate UWB-based localization accuracy under controlled conditions. Multiple anchor nodes were positioned in a calibrated grid, enabling sub-decimeter positional accuracy measurements. IMU data was synchronized to provide complementary inertial measurements for motion state classification.
WearableSensorsPage.localizationImageAlt: Workstation display showing a precision localization and tracking interface used in controlled indoor tests
WearableSensorsPage.localizationCaption: Controlled localization test environment with UWB anchor placement
WearableSensorsPage.motionText: Collected sensor data was processed through machine learning pipelines to classify motion states, detect anomalies such as falls, and evaluate athletic performance metrics. The fusion of UWB positional data and IMU acceleration/gyroscope readings enables robust discrimination between normal activity and fall events with high sensitivity and specificity.
WearableSensorsPage.motionImageAlt: Abstract visualization representing large-scale motion and sensor-derived data for analysis pipelines
WearableSensorsPage.motionCaption: Motion evaluation pipeline — sensor fusion and ML classification results
WearableSensorsPage.fieldText: Real-world trials were conducted in sports and healthcare settings to validate system performance outside controlled laboratory conditions. Field experiments tested the system's robustness to multipath propagation, body occlusion, and varying environmental conditions, demonstrating reliable fall detection and performance analysis in practical deployments.
WearableSensorsPage.fieldImageAlt: Athlete on an indoor court wearing ankle-mounted sensors during a motion capture style field experiment
WearableSensorsPage.fieldCaption: Field experiment setup — real-world wearable sensor deployment

metadata.title: Autonomous Underwater Gliders
metadata.description: Long-endurance autonomous platforms for underwater exploration and sensing — upcoming research by D7 Technology.

UnderwaterGlidersPage.title: Autonomous Underwater Gliders
UnderwaterGlidersPage.subtitle: Long-endurance autonomous platforms for underwater exploration and sensing
UnderwaterGlidersPage.description: Development of autonomous underwater glider platforms for long-endurance ocean monitoring, environmental data collection, and subsea sensing. Focuses on energy-efficient propulsion, navigation in dynamic underwater environments, and real-time data transmission.
